The '''R-1 recon droid''' was a standard-issue reconnaissance droid used by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. It was built by [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]] and was based on design of the [[DRK-1 Dark Eye probe droid/Legends|DRK-1 Dark Eye probe droid]], originally created by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. The R-1 was popular with [[Sniper/Legends|snipers]], planetary garrisons and shock troops. Some of them had also found a way into the hands of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], and other independent operations.

==Characteristics==
A small [[Repulsorlift/Legends|floating]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]], the [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]] R-1 [[Scout/Legends|recon]] [[Probe droid/Legends|droid]] lacked any tools or [[Limb/Legends|hands]], but could move through the openings that most of its potential targets could not. It could easily track possible targets, and its internal [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] allowed it to [[Death/Legends|kill]] a target if need be. The recon droid could be used to scout areas without risking a living observer, spot targets for [[Sniper/Legends|snipers]] or [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|Orbital bombardment]], seek out wanted criminals, or follow suspected individuals.<ref name="TFUCG" />

The R-1 recon droid was equipped with an internal [[orbital strike beacon]] with a high-power transmitter,<ref name="BF I" /> although the range was limited to one [[Planet/Legends|planet]].<ref name="TFUCG" /> Once active, friendly [[Starship/Legends|starships]] in orbit could lock onto the beacon's position and deliver a devastating, high-precision orbital strike. While not specifically intended as a self-destruct mechanism, per se, by using the beacon the droid was essentially calling in an orbital strike against its location, which it typically did not survive.<ref name="BF I" />

The droid's [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] were [[Color/Legends|red]] and had a black outer plating.<ref name="BF I" /> It was a fourth-degree droid and cost 16,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. It was based on a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[DRK-1 Dark Eye probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] design.<ref name="TFUCG" />

==History==
Arakyd manufactured the R-1 droids for use by the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. They were used by [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone]] [[Clone sharpshooter/Legends|snipers]] for scouting and target acquisition in a number of battlefronts.<ref name="BF I" /> During the post-Clone Wars rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], recon droids also saw use with planetary [[Garrison/Legends|garrisons]] and independent operators such as [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]].<ref name="TFUCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the [[2004]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]'', the sniper class is equipped with a remotely controllable [[Probe droid|recon droid]], which is capable of [[blaster cannon/Legends|blaster fire]] and calling down an [[Orbital bombardment|orbital strike]] on its position.

Although each faction's recon droid is functionally and aesthetically identical, they are classified as different models according to ''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Prima Official Game Guide]]''. Whereas the [[Battle droid assassin|assassin droid]] carries the [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd]] [[Mark IV recon droid]] and the [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout trooper]] and [[Rebel Marksman/Legends|Rebel marksman]] carry the Arakyd [[R-4 recon droid]], the [[Clone sharpshooter/Legends|clone sharpshooter]] is outfitted with an Arakyd R-1 recon droid. The recon droid returns in the sequel, ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'' (2005), as a part of the [[Galactic Marines/Legends|clone commander]], [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|MagnaGuard]], and [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]]'s arsenal instead, but ''[[Star Wars Battlefront II: Prima Official Game Guide]]'' designates them all as the Arakyd Mark IV recon droid.
